title: After lawsuit, Secretary of Commonwealth agrees to produce legally mandated report on voting in jail
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Secretary William Galvin agreed to complete reports on voting in jail after a lawsuit over missed deadlines.
The reports, required by the VOTES Act, will detail voting access for nearly 9,000 eligible incarcerated voters.
Galvin's office cited technical issues for the reporting delays and agreed to improve data processes before September elections.

title: New Patriots fullback Reggie Gilliam sees a similarity between Drake Maye and former MVP
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Reggie Gilliam joins the New England Patriots with a three-year contract after six years with the Buffalo Bills.
Gilliam compares new Patriots quarterback Drake Maye's build and potential to that of former MVP Josh Allen.
Gilliam aims to enhance the Patriots' run game, complementing Rhamondre Stevenson and TreVeyon Henderson in 2026.

title: Red Sox infielder Romy Gonzalez undergoes shoulder surgery; Boston signs lefty reliever ​
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Romy Gonzalez underwent shoulder surgery and will miss the first two months of the 2026 Red Sox season.
Boston Red Sox signed lefty reliever Danny Coulombe to a one-year contract for bullpen depth.
Coulombe posted a 2.30 ERA in 2025, split between the Twins and Rangers, with varied performance.

title: High school student allegedly killed Danvers woman in her home in ‘random’ attack, DA says
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Anthony DeMayo, a Bishop Fenwick High School senior, charged with murdering Janet Swallow, 68, in her Danvers home.
DeMayo allegedly found with a blood-stained knife; no known connection between suspect and victim, deemed a random attack.
DeMayo, from Lynn, to be arraigned Friday; crime deeply impacts Danvers, Bishop Fenwick community, and Swallow's family.

title: Boston police seeking suspect who allegedly assaulted pregnant woman in Roxbury laundromat
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Boston police seek public's help identifying suspect in alleged assault of pregnant woman in Roxbury laundromat.
Incident occurred at 11 a.m. on March 1 at 104 Warren St., suspect is a 6’0” male in Yankees hat.
Police request information on a woman present at the assault; tips can be submitted anonymously via CrimeStoppers.

title: Fired Mass. cop arrested in N.H. for allegedly impersonating a police officer
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Mikel Beltran, 37, was arrested in Londonderry, N.H. for allegedly impersonating a police officer with flashing blue lights.
Beltran was a former Lawrence police officer, fired in March 2025 after being placed on leave in September 2024.
State records allege Beltran set off a handgun on duty, made sexual remarks to a minor, and assaulted an individual.

title: NFL insider believes Eagles will lower asking price to Patriots in A.J. Brown trade
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: NFL insider Mike Giardi reported that the Eagles may lower their asking price for A.J. Brown in trade talks.
The Eagles initially requested a 2027 first-round pick and a 2026 second-round pick for Brown.
Trading Brown after June 1 saves the Eagles $7 million in cap space and reduces their dead cap hit.

title: Truck catches fire on Mass. Pike during early-morning commute, causing lane shutdowns
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: A truck fire occurred on the Massachusetts Turnpike in Westborough, prompting a shutdown of westbound lanes Thursday morning.
Firefighters from Westborough and Grafton extinguished the blaze by 8:30 a.m.; no injuries were reported.
The Massachusetts Department of Transportation reopened all lanes by 1:30 p.m.; the fire's cause remains under investigation.

title: New Patriots WR Romeo Doubs explains why he chose New England in free agency
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Romeo Doubs signed a four-year deal with the New England Patriots worth up to $80 million.
Doubs was Green Bay's top receiver in 2025, with 55 catches, 724 yards, and six touchdowns.
Doubs chose New England to work with QB Drake Maye and praised the Patriots' organizational culture.

title: SUV owner experiences a serious – and not uncommon – engine issue
author: John Paul
bullets: A 2018 Hyundai Tucson owner needed a full engine replacement after an engine failure on the highway.
Hyundai, Kia, Toyota, and Chevrolet models face engine issues due to bearing and manufacturing defects.
Hyundai replaced the reader's engine under warranty, highlighting a common defect in certain vehicle models.
